返回日报
Hacker News #5

谁在害怕中国模型？

Who's afraid of Chinese models?

AI 作者 mfiguiere 热度 843 回复 666 2026/07/20 19:05
打开原帖 打开外部链接 JSON

摘要

  • Stratechery 探讨中国AI模型（如 Kimi K3）接近国际最先进水平引发的行业讨论。
  • 文章指出AI时代边际成本重新成为关键因素，影响短期免费模型策略与长期产业结构。
  • 作者回顾聚合理论，认为AI正在让传统商业原则重新回归科技行业核心。

看点

  • Kimi K3 等中国开源权重模型能力逼近 state-of-the-art，引发 X 平台激烈讨论。
  • AI 模型边际成本回归，对免费模型和商业模式产生深远影响。
  • Stratechery 作者 Ben Thompson 从聚合理论视角分析AI对科技行业价值链的重塑。
  • 中美AI竞争背景下，中国模型快速进步引发行业关注与警惕。

文章讨论中国AI模型（如Kimi K3）的发展及其对行业结构的影响，涉及AI模型能力、边际成本、开源权重模型等科技与AI领域核心议题。

正文翻译

暂无正文

暂无原文

回复翻译 20

softwaredoug 2026/07/22 01:50
我没有意识到的事情。白宫指示情报机构帮助防止美国模型的蒸馏。

无论蒸馏量是小还是大，仍然很有趣：

https://www.whitehouse.gov/presidential-actions/2026/06/nati...

Something I did not realize. The White House instructed intelligence agencies to help in preventing distillation of US models.

Whether or not distillation matters a small amount or a big amount, still interesting:

https://www.whitehouse.gov/presidential-actions/2026/06/nati...

楼层
sperr11 2026/07/22 01:34
我认为一个简单的实验就足以理解为什么人们会对国家审查的人工智能模型有所担忧：只需尝试询问他们国家所犯下的暴行[1]。

[1]：https://imgur.com/gallery/ai-models-on-atrocities-B7DKUXc

I think a simple experiment is enough to understand why one would have some concern with a state-censored AI model : Just try asking them about atrocities committed by their state[1].

[1] : https://imgur.com/gallery/ai-models-on-atrocities-B7DKUXc

楼层
NyxWulf 2026/07/22 01:42
这一点的重要性怎么强调都不为过。 如果说中国是开放的标准，那它的标准相当低。 我认为你的观点从不同的角度补充了文章的内容，但到达了相似的地方。 我们最好的自我在很多方面都是由开放、坚定的自我反思和竞争来定义的。 我们应该记住这一点，正如文章作者所说，要倾向于它，而不是让恐惧散布和表演来保护这些模型免受竞争。
It's hard to overstate how important this point is. If China is the standard of openness, it's a pretty low standard. Your point I think adds to what the article is saying, from a different perspective, but arrives at a similar place. Our best selves in many ways are defined by openness, unflinching self reflection, and competition. We should remember that, and as the author of the article says, lean into it rather than letting fear mongering and histrionics protect these models from competition.

samiv 2026/07/21 23:37
我不敢相信来自在流氓侵略国家管辖下运营的公司的技术，该国家不断利用经济和军事行动攻击其他国家（所谓的）盟友和敌人。
I'm afraid to trust technology coming from companies operating under the jurisdiction of a rogue aggressive nation that is continuously attacking other nations both (so called) ally and foe using economic and military actions.

Really? Ok, name list of rogue aggressive nations that are continuously attacking other nations both allies and foes, and I tell you which one he meant...

bigyabai 2026/07/22 01:18

Are you describing the United States or China with this quote? It's hard to tell.

tcmart14 2026/07/22 01:31
除了与印度的一些边境争端外，自从中越战争（1979年）被越南打得落花流水以来，我认为中国没有对任何人发动过军事攻击。所以我认为这排除了中国。
Outside of a few boarder disputes with India, I don't China has militarily attacked anyone since they got their ass handed to them by Vietnam (Sino-Vietnamese War 1979). So I think that rules out China.

楼层
gigatexal 2026/07/21 23:46
我并不是说习政府和共产主义中国的铁腕手段等同于特朗普2.0政府那种彻头彻尾的无法无天、腐败、欺诈、报复心重且充满仇恨……但这对我来说简直就像 * 孩子们做的那个6和7的手势 * 一样。

中国只会做得更好——如果他们真这么做的话——在存储、整理、索引和使用其国家赞助和推崇的AI实验室的数据方面，利用这些数据来对付美国。[1]

而本届美国政府也在做同样的事，攻击大学、盟友，甚至本国公民。

两国政府的行事方式大同小异。因此，两国AI公司的AI模型同样不值得信任。

[1] https://www.abc.net.au/news/2019-05-16/grindr-why-is-the-us-... 已故的林赛·格雷厄姆浮现在脑海中。可能还有更大的重磅炸弹。

Not saying the heavy hand of the Xi admin and Chinese communism is equivalent to the outright lawless, corrupt, grifting and vindictive and hateful administration that is Trump 2.0 ... but it's pretty much * makes the 6 7 motion that the kids do * this for me.

China will just do a better job -- if they do this at all -- of storing, collating, indexing, and using data from their state sponsored and championed AI labs to use that against the US. [1]

The US under this admin is doing the same, attacking universities, allies, it's own citizens.

The two governments are operating more or less the same. Ergo the ai models from each country's ai companies shant be trusted either.

[1] https://www.abc.net.au/news/2019-05-16/grindr-why-is-the-us-... The late Lindsay Graham comes to mind. Even larger bombshells probably.

楼层
astrobiased 2026/07/21 18:40
这篇文章切中要害，在安全性方面对模型的访问不平等。我正在开发 OSS，其中安全性对用户来说很重要......但是像 GPT 5.6 和 Fable 这样的前沿模型会崩溃并声明我无法获取信息/访问权限。

这是极其不平衡的，我必须诉诸 GLM 5.2/K3 来确保这些安全问题（希望）得到正确解决。

对于 OSS，这是我经历过的最违反直觉的体验之一。我比以往任何时候都更加相信开放权重和开放管道模型对于人工智能和社会前沿的进步 100% 至关重要。

The post hits it spot on with unequal access to the models in terms of security. I'm developing OSS where security is important for the user ... but the frontier models like GPT 5.6 and Fable flake out and state that I cannot get the info/access.

This is extremely lopsided I'll have to resort to GLM 5.2/K3 to ensure that those security issues (hopefully) are resolved properly.

For OSS, this is one of the most counterintuitive experiences I have ever had. More than ever I'm convinced that open weight and open pipelines models are 100% critical for progress on the AI and societal fronts.

楼层
bigyabai 2026/07/22 01:28
我部分在想，美国政府是不是在封杀Anthropic和OpenAI的嘴，以便他们囤积NOBUS漏洞利用： https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NOBUS

如果这些模型能用来修补（或发现）危险的有效载荷，那么限制这些模型就会有相当大的动机。在像Windows或Chrome这样的大型项目中，可能仍然有数十个未修补的漏洞，这些漏洞太微妙，较小的模型无法捕捉到。

Part of me wonders if the US Government is muzzling Anthropic and OpenAI so they can stockpile NOBUS exploits: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NOBUS

There would be a decently large incentive to restrict these models if they could be used to patch (or discover) dangerous payloads. In larger projects like Windows or Chrome, there might still be dozens of unpatched exploits that are too subtle to catch with smaller models.

楼层
nateburke 2026/07/22 00:33
发布可以接近前沿水平智能的开放权重（无论销毁的代币数量如何）只是告诉世界，任何人，甚至中国，只要拥有芯片和温暖的外壳，都可以提供前沿水平的推理服务。

那么，在服务推理方面，是什么阻止了中国获得多数市场份额？

人工智能主权 --是

网络安全问题 - 是

延迟 - 否，与以前新兴的 IT 工作负载类型不同，推理没有很强的延迟要求。例如，1 秒的额外网络延迟对于 15 分钟、10 回合的代理会话来说并不重要。

成本 - 最终这取决于一个国家将芯片插入温暖外壳的能力。它在芯片方面涉及地缘政治/贸易，在热壳方面涉及能源可扩展性和模块化。即使你认为地缘政治/贸易是一个难以抉择的问题，中国在能源方面已经轻松击败了美国，他们每年向电网部署的电力是美国的 10 倍，而美国在每一个可能的时刻都会搬起石头砸自己的脚。

恕我直言，芯片技术将跨越国界，如果模拟推理没有突破，能源可扩展性最终将占据主导地位。

人们需要记住两件事：

1) 中国可以（并且确实）使用这些模型来影响西方。他们接受有关台湾和香港的虚假信息的训练。或者假装历史对中国有利。

2）忽略包含虚假信息的模型，它们是令人难以置信的。但您应该害怕直接通过模型创建者运行推理。如果您认为您的数据与通过美国的模型提供商（无论是前沿还是像 fireworks.ai 这样的模型主机）运行相比是安全的，那么请让我知道您的银行详细信息，以便我可以四处查看。

这一点的重要性怎么强调都不为过。 如果说中国是开放的标准，那它的标准相当低。 我认为你的观点从不同的角度补充了文章的内容，但到达了相似的地方。 我们最好的自我在很多方面都是由开放、坚定的自我反思和竞争来定义的。 我们应该记住这一点，正如文章作者所说，要倾向于它，而不是让恐惧散布和表演来保护这些模型免受竞争。 先生，考虑到当今世界正在发生的事情，我真的无法判断您的声明指的是哪个国家：D
Releasing open weights that can approach frontier level intelligence (irrespective of number of tokens burned) is just a way of telling the world that anyone, even China, can serve frontier level inference if they have the chips and warm shells to do so.

What is stopping China from gaining a majority market share, then, in terms of serving inference?

AI Sovereignty -- yes

Cybersecurity concerns -- yes

Latency -- no, unlike previous emerging IT workload types , inference does not have strong latency requirements. eg 1s of additional network latency doesn't matter to a 15 min, 10-turn agent session.

Cost -- ultimately this comes down to a nations ability to plug chips into warm shells. which forks into geopolitical / trade on the chips side and energy scalability and modularity on the warm-shell side. Even if you call geopolitical / trade a toss-up, China has the US beat HANDILY on the energy front, yearly they are deploying 10x power to their grid relative to the US, which is shooting itself in the foot at every possible moment.

IMHO chip tech will travel across borders, absent a breakthrough in analog inference, energy scalability will ultimately dominate.

楼层
ballon_monkey 2026/07/21 11:18
人们需要记住两件事：

1) 中国可以（并且确实）使用这些模型来影响西方。他们接受有关台湾和香港的虚假信息的训练。或者假装历史对中国有利。

2）忽略包含虚假信息的模型，它们是令人难以置信的。但您应该害怕直接通过模型创建者运行推理。如果您认为您的数据与通过美国的模型提供商（无论是前沿还是像 fireworks.ai 这样的模型主机）运行相比是安全的，那么请让我知道您的银行详细信息，以便我可以四处查看。

The 2 things people need to remember:

1) China can (and does) use the models to influence the west. They train in false information about Taiwan and Hong Kong. Or pretend like history is in favor of China.

2) Ignoring the models containing false information, they are incredible. But you should be scared of running inference via the model creators directly. If you think your data is safe compared to running it via model providers in the US ( either frontier or model hosts like fireworks.ai ) then please let me know your bank details so I can poke around.

楼层
w4lker 2026/07/21 17:32

我为什么要更信任一家美国公司而不是中国公司呢？

https://thereallo.dev/blog/claude-code-prompt-steganography

Why should I trust a US company more than a Chinese one?

楼层
theptip 2026/07/21 22:57
您的展品明显是一种反蒸馏技术。在监管较松的公司那里，情况可能会糟糕得多。
Your exhibit is an obvious anti-distillation technique. You can expect far worse from less-regulated companies.
楼层
